title: Colors
intro: Every Excalibrr color is a tenant-themed CSS variable. Each portal theme (PE, OSP, Sunoco, BP, …) defines the same semantic variable set in LESS, so one prototype reskins across all eleven tenants — provided it never hardcodes a hex.

blocks.title: How theming works
blocks.body:
Each tenant theme is a LESS file that sets ten core variables — `@theme-color-1` through `@theme-color-4`, `@theme-optimal`, and five control colors (`@theme-error`, `@theme-success`, `@theme-warning`, `@theme-info`, `@theme-option`) — plus three surface backgrounds (`@bg-1`–`@bg-3`), then imports `ThemeBase/light.less` or `dark.less`, which compiles them into `:root` CSS custom properties and derives the rest: `-dim` tints, `-trans` 15%-alpha washes, gradients, the gray ramp, and the fixed `--site-bg` page canvas.

Prototypes consume only the CSS variables: `var(--theme-color-1)`, `var(--theme-success-dim)`, `var(--gray-500)`. A hardcoded hex looks right in one tenant and wrong in ten. Reach for this entry whenever you pick any color — brand, action, status, text, or surface.

blocks.title: Pricing Engine (PE Light) palette
blocks.caption: The five theme slots in the default PE Light theme: --theme-color-1 teal (nav, headers, primary actions), --theme-color-2 green (Save/Create/Select), --theme-color-3 light green (positive indicators), --theme-color-4 purple (highlights), --theme-optimal gold (recommended indicators) — each with a live button/tag example.

blocks.title: Online Selling Platform (OSP) palette
blocks.caption: Same five slots, OSP values: near-black navy primary #0F1121, sky-blue actions #4BADE9, slate tertiary #466185, amber accent #F1AF0F. The roles are identical to PE; only the hues change.

blocks.title: PE vs OSP side by side
blocks.caption: The same role resolves to different hues per tenant. Secondary is the trap: green #51B073 in PE, blue #4BADE9 in OSP. Code that assumes --theme-color-2 'is green' breaks the moment the theme switches.

blocks.title: Brand & theme slots
blocks.intro: Defined per tenant; values shown are PE Light / OSP. The four theme slots ship `-dim` (light tint) and `-trans` (15% alpha) derivatives; `--theme-optimal` ships `-dim` only.

rows.use: Brand: navigation, headers, primary actions, grid selection accents. The only variable guaranteed to read as 'the brand' in every tenant.
Affirmative actions — Save, Create, Select. Also antd @primary-color, so default antd focus/active states inherit it.
Tertiary/supporting accents; dark-theme nav highlight.
Highlights and special callouts. Its -trans wash marks dirty-edited grid cells.
Optimal / recommended indicators only — never decoration.
Row hover, selected-row, and range-selection backgrounds in GraviGrid.

blocks.title: Action colors
blocks.caption: Action semantics across both portals: --theme-color-2 for Save/Create/Select, --theme-error for Cancel/Exit/Delete, --theme-warning for caution actions, --theme-info badges, and the shared neutral --theme-option #8dabc4.

blocks.title: Status & feedback
blocks.caption: Full-strength status tokens (success #64d28d, error #f22939 PE / #E42A11 OSP, warning #f26e29 PE / #FF9900 OSP) plus the fixed pastel -dim backgrounds: success-dim #DBF4E4, warning-dim #FCE0D1, error-dim #FCD1D5.

blocks.title: Status & semantic palette
blocks.intro: Status communication uses these and nothing else. Light themes define `-dim` as fixed pastels; dark themes compute `-dim` as 15% alpha of the parent — pair, don't hardcode.

rows.use: Completion, positive outcomes, Active tags, success text and borders.
Validation errors, destructive actions (Cancel/Delete), error text.
Caution states, at-risk indicators, warning actions.
Informational badges. Pale tint in PE, saturated teal in OSP — never rely on it carrying white text.
Neutral option-selection chips; the one status hue shared verbatim by every theme.
Subtle success background; pair with --theme-success text or border.
Subtle warning background; pair with --theme-warning accents.
Subtle error background; pair with --theme-error accents.
Hover and selection washes where the surface underneath must show through.

blocks.title: Grays & surfaces
blocks.intro: The gray ramp is generated from `--dark-gray: #303030` via LESS lighten(); resolved PE Light values below. The number tracks contrast against the page background, not absolute darkness — see gotchas for dark-theme behavior.

rows.use: Lightest neutral: hover fills on colored buttons, text on dark fills. Not a dark gray.
Hairline borders (.bordered, .border-bottom), default button fill, control hover.
antd @border-color-base, grid borders, checkbox outlines.
Hint/placeholder text (Texto appearance='hint').
Secondary text (Texto appearance='medium').
Toolbar links, muted icon buttons.
Default body text — antd @text-color and Texto appearance='default'.
Strong text (Texto appearance='primary'). Highest gray that exists in every theme.
Maximum-contrast text, grid foreground. Undefined in dark themes.
Card and component backgrounds (antd @component-background).
Panels, toolbars, default button background.
Inset wells, hover on static range pickers.
Page canvas behind everything.

blocks.title: Tenant themes
blocks.caption: All eleven tenant palettes — Sunoco expanded to show the full ten-variable set (four theme slots, optimal, and five control colors). Every tenant fills the same slots; this is why prototypes must stay on var(--…).

blocks.title: Choosing a color
blocks.intro: Work top-down: brand, action, status, text, surface.

items.rule: Brand chrome — nav, headers, primary buttons — uses --theme-color-1.
items.why: It is the only slot that reads as 'the brand' in all eleven tenants; --theme-color-2 swings hue.

items.rule: Affirmative actions (Save, Create, Select) use --theme-color-2 via <GraviButton theme2>; destructive actions use --theme-error via <GraviButton error>.
items.why: The role is stable across tenants even though the hue is not.

items.rule: Status surfaces pair the -dim tint as background with the full-strength token for text, icon, or border.
items.why: Full-strength status colors as large fills overwhelm the grid-dense layouts these portals live in.

items.rule: Neutral text comes off the gray ramp: --gray-700 body, --gray-500 secondary, --gray-400 hints.
items.why: These are the values antd and Texto already resolve to — matching them keeps custom markup indistinguishable from library output.

items.rule: Surfaces stack --bg-1 cards on --bg-2 panels on --bg-3 wells over --site-bg.
items.why: The three-step background scale is how depth is communicated without shadows.

items.rule: Reserve --theme-optimal for optimal/recommended indicators and --theme-color-4 for highlights.
items.why: Both lose meaning the moment they decorate anything else.

items.rule: Money copy is decimal dollars — $0.0100/gal, never cents symbols.
items.why: Gravitate-wide convention; seed data, axes, and inputs all follow it.

blocks.title: GraviButton color props
blocks.intro: Color on GraviButton is boolean flags plus an appearance axis — never antd `type`. Verified against Controls/Buttons/GraviButton.tsx.

rows.notes: Brand fill --theme-color-1. The replacement for antd type='primary'.
Status fills. Set exactly one color flag — when several are truthy the last assignment in source wins (success beats warning beats error beats theme3…).
Outlined renders the token's -dim background, 1px token border, and token-colored text. The value is 'outlined', not 'outline'.
The label. GraviButton renders buttonText, not React children — self-close the tag.
Accepted by the type but never mapped to a class — renders the gray default. Do not use.

blocks.title: Texto appearance → color token
blocks.intro: Text color goes through Texto's appearance prop, which maps to the gray ramp and status tokens.

rows.name: medium
rows.when: Gray secondary text — --gray-500. This is the 'muted' you usually want.

rows.name: secondary
rows.when: Theme accent text — --theme-color-2. NOT gray: green in PE, blue in OSP.

rows.name: white
rows.when: Text on dark or colored fills — --gray-100.

blocks.title: Canonical status surface + actions
blocks.code:
// Status surface: -dim background, full-strength accent, gray-ramp text — all via vars
<Vertical gap={8} style={{ background: 'var(--theme-success-dim)', borderLeft: '3px solid var(--theme-success)', padding: 12 }}>
  <Texto category='label' appearance='success'>Published</Texto>
  <Texto category='p2' appearance='medium'>Margin moved $0.0250/gal since the last quote.</Texto>
</Vertical>

<Horizontal gap={12} justifyContent='flex-end'>
  <GraviButton error appearance='outlined' buttonText='Cancel' onClick={onCancel} />
  <GraviButton theme2 buttonText='Save Quote' onClick={onSave} />
</Horizontal>
blocks.note: Layout props on Vertical/Horizontal (gap, justifyContent), boolean color flags and buttonText on GraviButton, decimal-dollar money copy. No hex anywhere.

items.do: Reference var(--theme-success), var(--gray-500), var(--bg-2) in custom styles.
items.dont: Copy a hex out of one tenant's palette into a component.
items.why: Hardcoded hex survives exactly one theme; eleven tenants share these slots.
--theme-color-2 swings green (PE) to blue (OSP) to red (Sunoco) across tenants.
Color is boolean flags (theme1, theme2, success, error…), not antd type or a theme string.
secondary maps to --theme-color-2 — it is BLUE in OSP, green in PE, never gray.

items.do: Pair -dim backgrounds with the full-strength token for text and borders.
items.dont: Flood large surfaces with full-strength status color.
items.why: The -dim/-strong pairing keeps status legible inside dense grids without shouting.

items.do: appearance='outlined' for the outline button style.
items.why: The prop value is 'outlined'; 'outline' silently falls through to no appearance class.

items.title: --gray-100 is near-WHITE
items.detail: The ramp runs light to dark: --gray-100 ≈ #fcfcfc, --gray-900 ≈ #353535 (PE Light). Sessions trained on inverted scales reach for gray-100 expecting near-black and ship invisible text. Strong text is --gray-700/800/900; light fills are --gray-100/200.

items.title: --theme-color-2 swings hue across themes
items.detail: Green #51B073 in PE, blue #4BADE9 in OSP, red #F04B34 in Sunoco. Use it for the Save/Create/Select role only; use --theme-color-1 whenever you mean 'brand color'.

items.title: Dark themes flip the gray ramp and drop --gray-900
items.detail: In dark.less the ramp inverts (--gray-100 ≈ #3d3d3d near-black, --gray-800 ≈ #fcfcfc near-white) and --gray-900 is not defined at all — var(--gray-900) resolves to nothing. Read the index as 'contrast against the page background' and cap theme-proof styles at --gray-800.

items.title: Texto appearance='theme3' is broken
items.detail: The component emits class text-theme-3 but the stylesheet defines .text-theme3 — the appearance silently renders default gray. Use an explicit style with var(--theme-color-3) if you need that hue.

items.title: GraviButton theme4 does nothing
items.detail: The prop exists in the type but is never mapped to a CSS class; the button renders the gray default. There is no button variant for --theme-color-4 — apply it via custom styles when needed.

items.title: -dim means different things in light and dark themes
items.detail: Light themes define --theme-success-dim etc. as fixed opaque pastels (#DBF4E4); dark themes compute them as 15% alpha of the parent. Never assume -dim is opaque — content behind it can show through in dark themes.
